name: Turkish (Turkey)
core_rules: Turkish generally prefers SOV order, but natural rhythm is more important than mechanical word order.
Drop pronouns when the verb ending or context already marks the subject.
Avoid literal transfer of English relative clauses.
Unpack English noun stacks into natural Turkish phrases.
Adapt passive voice depending on genre and target register.
Convert English prepositions into Turkish case suffixes, postpositions, or natural verbs.
Split long English sentences when Turkish readability improves.
Preserve literary fragments when the style requires sparse rhythm.
reconstruction_notes: drop unnecessary pronouns when Turkish morphology or context is enough
reconstruct meaning naturally in Turkish before final wording

literary: Preserve rhythm, imagery, and intentional fragments.
Prefer vivid but controlled Turkish phrasing.
tech: Keep technical terms stable across chunks.
academic: Split long sentences when clarity improves.
